title: 10 best herbal essential oils for lost voice
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for lost voice are a natural remedy that helps to soothe and restore vocal cords. These oils work by reducing inflammation, relaxing the throat, and promoting healthy tissue growth. The best essential oils for lost voice include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Lavandula angustifolia, Thymus serpyllum, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Trifolium pratense. If left untreated, a lost voice can significantly impact daily life, making communication and social interactions challenging, and even affecting one's profession or career, leading to feelings of isolation and frustration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower essential oils helps with lost voice because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as eudesmanolide and isoalantolactone, work together to soothe irritated vocal cords and reduce inflammation caused by respiratory infections or strain. This natural remedy helps to calm the throat and restore vocal function, making it an effective treatment for lost voice due to colds, laryngitis, or vocal cord strain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, which can help soothe a lost voice caused by laryngitis or vocal strain.
Antioxidant property: Helps protect the mucous membranes of the throat and vocal cords from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healing and recovery of the voice.

Immune-boosting property: Supports the immune system's natural defenses against infection and inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of lost voice caused by respiratory infections or other underlying conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help soothe and calm the throat, promoting a healthy vocal cord.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate inflammation and pain associated with a lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers on a sunny morning at dawn.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 days, stirring occasionally.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a clevenger apparatus with 100 ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and collect 10 ml of echinacea purpurea essential oil.
Transfer the collected essential oil to dark glass bottles and store in the refrigerator at 2-3°c temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with lost voice because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's flavonoids and terpenes work together to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, allowing the vocal cords to function properly again. Additionally, the oil's antibacterial and antiviral properties help combat infections that can cause a lost voice. By using maidenhair tree essential oils, individuals can promote relaxation, ease congestion, and stimulate the healing process, ultimately helping to restore their natural speaking voice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to a lost voice.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of Maidenhair Tree Essential Oil helps protect the vocal cords from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate a lost voice.
Vasodilatory properties: Ginkgo biloba essential oil can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation to the throat and larynx, which may aid in restoring normal vocal function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a rich concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help soothe vocal cord irritation.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from a healthy plant with 100-200g weight.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves with water at 1:5 ratio and 100°c temperature for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation mixture by decantation and filtering through paper filter with 0.2 micron pore size.
Store the clear golden-colored essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with lost voice because of its soothing and calming properties, which can help to relax the vocal cords and reduce inflammation. The oil's antimicrobial properties can also help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the loss of voice. Additionally, the gentle and non-irritating nature of English lavender oil makes it an ideal remedy for sensitive throats, allowing it to provide effective relief without causing further discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the vocal cords, allowing for smoother and clearer speech.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of lavender essential oil can help combat infections and inflammation in the throat, reducing the risk of further strain on the vocal cords and promoting a quicker recovery.

Soothing and Calming: Lavender essential oil has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often exacerbate lost voice or vocal strain, allowing for relaxation and reduced tension in the throat and vocal cords.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which can help soothe and calm the throat, making it effective in treating lost voice.

Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool, a compound known for its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, promoting recovery from lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from a mature plant in full bloom, ensuring optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air-drying method, taking approximately 24-48 hours to achieve crisp dryness.
Crush the dried lavender flowers into small pieces to release their essential oils, using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for efficient processing.
Steam-distill the crushed lavender flowers with water in a dedicated still, aiming for a ratio of one part plant material to 5 parts water, over a period of 1-2 hours to capture the precious oil.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil into dark glass bottles, taking care to preserve its delicate aroma and therapeutic properties within a few days of production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated vocal cords. The oil's antimicrobial qualities also aid in reducing inflammation and congestion, allowing for a smoother and more consistent flow of air through the throat and mouth. Additionally, thyme essential oil's decongestant properties can help to reduce swelling and open up airways, promoting a clear and strong voice.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thyme essential oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to a lost voice, such as laryngitis or tonsillitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for a smoother and more effective vocal performance.

Antioxidant property: Thyme essential oil is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the delicate tissues of the throat and vocal cords from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to vocal strain and loss of voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are often used to create essential oils due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to soothe and calm the vocal cords.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are a popular choice for making essential oils, as they contain flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat and vocal cords.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from well-established plants grown in full sun.
Dry the thyme leaves in a single layer on paper bags or wire racks for 7 days.
Steam distill 100g of dried thyme leaves in 500ml of water at 60°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through coffee filters or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles.
Store the thymus vulgaris essential oil in a cool place away from direct sunlight and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with lost voice because of its natural properties that stimulate blood flow to the vocal cords, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation. The antimicrobial properties also help to combat bacterial infections that may cause hoarseness or loss of voice. Additionally, rosemary's ability to improve circulation can help to loosen mucus and reduce congestion, allowing for clearer communication and a restored voice. By incorporating rosemary essential oils into your daily routine, you can promote vocal health and regain your lost voice.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation in the throat, which can contribute to a hoarse or lost voice by soothing and protecting the mucous membranes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary essential oil can help reduce swelling and redness in the throat, making it easier to produce vocal cords and recover from vocal strain.
Stimulant properties: Rosemary essential oil's stimulant properties can help increase blood flow to the throat and vocal cords, which can help improve vocal function and reduce hoarseness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming the vocal cords.
Stems: Rosemary stems contain a high concentration of camphor, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the larynx, alleviating hoarseness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning, ensuring they are free of any signs of disease or damage.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with distilled water and let them air dry for at least 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Place the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a glass jar filled with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3, ensuring the lid is securely tightened.
Store the infused mixture in a cool, dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction and diffusion.

Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the resulting essential oil, which can be used immediately or stored in an amber glass bottle for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with lost voice because of its soothing and calming properties. It contains rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce throat irritation and discomfort. The oil also possesses antiviral and antibacterial properties that can aid in combating infections that may be causing the loss of voice. When used in a steam inhalation or applied topically to the throat, lemon balm essential oils can help ease congestion, reduce swelling, and promote healthy vocal cord function, ultimately helping to restore lost voice.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil helps combat viral infections such as laryngitis, which can cause a loss of voice, by targeting and eliminating the virus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the vocal cords, allowing for smoother airflow and improved vocal cord function, thus helping to restore lost voice.

Sedative and calming effects: The calming properties of lemon balm essential oil help soothe an irritated throat and calm anxiety, which can exacerbate a loss of voice by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are known for their calming and soothing properties, making them effective in relieving vocal cord tension and inflammation associated with lost voice.
Flowers: The fragrant flowers of lemon balm contain compounds that help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to vocal strain and loss of voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when their aroma is most potent and distill immediately.
Steam-distill 150g of fresh melissa officinalis leaves in 500ml of distilled water for 30-40 minutes.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities within an hour.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to one month.
Mix 10 drops of melissa officinalis essential oil with 1 tablespoon of sweet almond oil and apply topically to the throat area three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When inhaled, it calms irritated mucous membranes and reduces inflammation in the throat, allowing for proper vocal cord function to resume. Additionally, hyssop's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, further reducing congestion and discomfort that can contribute to a lost voice. remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Hyssop essential oil helps to soothe and calm coughs, which can aid in recovering a lost voice by reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat.

Anti-inflammatory property: Hyssop essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and redness in the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, promoting a healthier environment for the recovery of a lost voice.

Antimicrobial property: Hyssop essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help to fight off infections such as laryngitis, which is often the underlying cause of a lost voice, by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated throats, promoting a healthy recovery from lost voice.
Buds: The buds contain bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and congestion, allowing for a smoother and clearer flow of air through the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated using scissors cutting stems just above node.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm air circulation drying cabinet at 40°c for 24 hours or until brittle and crumbly.
Steam distill the dried leaves with water in a copper still at 100°c pressure and collect distilled essential oil in a separating funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to six months.
Use two drops of the essential oil mixed with carrier oil or saline solution three times daily to soothe and calm the throat during lost voice episodes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the throat. The oil's gentle astringent quality also helps to reduce swelling and redness, allowing for smoother vocal cord function. Additionally, the oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, promoting clearer breathing and improved vocal projection. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm the vocal cords, making it an effective part for treating lost voice.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves from mature trees in late morning or early evening for optimal oil content.
Dry the eucalyptus leaves thoroughly using shade drying or dehydrator on low setting for 24 hours.
Steam distill dried eucalyptus leaves with water at a ratio of 1:4 in a stainless steel still for 2-3 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil from the condenser coil, discarding any sediment or impurities.
Store the finished eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh or dried calendula flowers on a sunny day.
Dry the calendula flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for seven days.
Steam distill 50 grams of dried calendula flowers using 500 milliliters of distilled water for 2 hours.
Filter and decant the essential oil into a dark glass bottle at room temperature within an hour.
Store the calendula officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for up to six months with a shelf life of two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with lost voice because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the vocal cords, reducing swelling and redness. remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare aerial parts at peak flowering stage, collecting approximately 1 kilogram of fresh material.
Wash the harvested foeniculum vulgare with cold running water and gently shake off excess moisture.
Steam distill the washed foeniculum vulgare aerial parts using a continuous still for 4-5 hours, yielding 10 ml of essential oil.
Filter the obtained essential oil through a sterile coffee filter or paper to remove any impurities or suspended particles.
Store the filtered foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep in a cool place for up to 1 year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat lost voice with herbal essential oils is by blending a few drops of soothing essentials like lavender, peppermint, and eucalyptus with a carrier oil. Gently massage the blend onto your throat and neck area to help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Additionally, inhaling the vapors from these oils through steam inhalation or aromatherapy can also ease hoarseness and restore your voice.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lost voice that are treated with herbal essential oils are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and laryngitis. Essential oils like eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme help to ease congestion and inflammation in the throat and lungs, promoting clear breathing and soothing a sore or raspy voice. Additionally, these oils can also alleviate symptoms of allergies and sinus infections that may cause hoarseness or loss of voice.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with lost voice because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the vocal cords. The oil's expectorant properties also help to clear mucus and reduce swelling, allowing the voice to return to normal. Additionally, its antioxidant properties protect the vocal cords from damage caused by free radicals. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil a popular natural remedy for vocal strain and loss of voice, often used by singers, public speakers, and performers to maintain their vocal health and prevent voice loss.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, thereby alleviating the symptoms of lost voice.
Flavonoids: As antioxidants, flavonoids protect the vocal cords from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy voice production and recovery from lost voice.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which help calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with lost voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm the vocal cords.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other beneficial compounds.
Barks: The barks of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant are sometimes used to create essential oils, which may help to relieve throat inflammation and promote a healthy vocal tone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning when they are at their highest concentration of bioactive compounds.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris then steam distill them for 2 hours.
Distill the steam distillate using a fractional distillation apparatus to separate the essential oil from water for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities then store it in a dark glass bottle.
Allow the essential oil to mature for 2 weeks before using it to ensure its quality and stability.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with lost voice because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for proper mucus drainage and healing. Its antimicrobial properties also combat infections that cause vocal strain and inflammation. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties protect the throat and vocal cords from oxidative stress, promoting a healthy environment for vocal cords to recover and regain their natural resonance. Alkylamides: These alkylamide compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable recovery.
Icariin: This flavonoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the throat from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea contain compounds that help in soothing the throat and reducing inflammation, making them suitable for lost voice.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the vocal cords, making them useful for essential oils for lost voice.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Steam distill the harvested material in a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container for a period of 4-6 hours.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and debris.
Store the final echinacea purpurea essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the throat. The oil's warming and comforting effects help to relax the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Its antimicrobial properties also help to combat infections that can cause a lost voice, promoting a healthy and smooth vocal flow. Regular use of ginger essential oil may also help to improve vocal cord function and prevent future instances of lost voice.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the inflamed vocal cords and reduce swelling, thereby alleviating lost voice.

Phenolic compounds (such as gingerols and shogaols): These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation in the throat, allowing the voice to recover.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the vocal cords from further damage and promote healing, thus aiding in the recovery of lost voice.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make essential oils for lost voice because they contain high concentrations of the active compound, gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and warm the vocal cords.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils for lost voice because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the vocal cords.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes when mature and dry them completely in a warm place for 2 to 3 days.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes in a still at 100°c to 150°c for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the distilled vapor and separate it from the water using a condenser coil and a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the extracted zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle and keep it in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with lost voice because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and calm the vocal cords. Carvacrol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the throat, reduce swelling, and promote healing, which can help restore a person's voice.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, which can help alleviate discomfort and promote vocal cord healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of thyme essential oil, which is often used to relieve respiratory issues and soothe a lost voice.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present for 1-2 hours.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer for 7-10 days in a well-ventilated area to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried plant material at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separating funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with lost voice because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. α-Pinene: It has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus that may be blocking the vocal cords and preventing voice production.
Cineole: It has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, reducing swelling and congestion in the throat and vocal cords, which can help to restore a lost voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a primary compound responsible for the oil's decongestant and expectorant properties.
Buds: The buds contain a higher concentration of eucalyptol compared to the leaves and are often used to create more potent essential oils for respiratory issues.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus contains a higher concentration of globulin, a compound that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, making it useful for treating lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves using a distillation apparatus with 500 milliliters of water for 2 hours.
Separate the distilled essential oil from the water and leaves using a separatory funnel and collect 20 milliliters of oil.
Filter the collected essential oil using a 0.2 micrometer filter paper to remove any impurities and achieve a clear oil consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with lost voice because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Menthone: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat and vocal cords, which can become clogged and cause lost voice.

Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and irritation in the vocal cords, and also has expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and promote healthy voice production.

Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.

Additionally, its relaxing effects on the mind and body can help to reduce stress and anxiety, allowing the vocal cords to recover and regain their full range and clarity. This can help to alleviate the discomfort and restore the voice.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and discomfort that may be contributing to a lost voice.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may be exacerbating a lost voice, allowing the vocal cords to recover and function properly.

Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the vocal cords from damage and promote healing, reducing the risk of further vocal cord strain and promoting a return to normal voice function.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils for lost voice as they possess calming and soothing properties that can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the throat.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for lost voice as they contain antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing in the throat.
Stems: The stems are used to make essential oils for lost voice as they contain flavonoids and terpenes that can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowering tops of lavandula angustifolia plants in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for 30-40 days.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator at 35-40°c for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content to 10-15%.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a 10:1 water to herb ratio at 100°c for 2-3 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it using a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid away from heat and light.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum essential oils helps with lost voice because of its unique properties. The oil is rich in thymol, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. This makes it an effective natural remedy for soothing a sore throat and reducing inflammation in the vocal cords. As a result, Thymus serpyllum essential oils can help restore a person's lost voice by reducing swelling and promoting healing in the throat, ultimately allowing them to speak and sing with ease once again. It's a natural solution to get back to speaking and singing with confidence.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps to soothe the vocal cords and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with lost voice.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections and inflammation in the throat, promoting a healthy environment for vocal cords to function properly.
Cineole: Has anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing swelling and congestion in the throat, making it easier to speak and maintain a healthy voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus serpyllum leaves are used to make essential oils because they contain thymol, a compound that helps soothe the throat and promote vocal cord health.
Flowers: Thymus serpyllum flowers are used to make essential oils because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and promote a healthy voice.
Roots: Thymus serpyllum roots are used to make essential oils because they contain antioxidants that help protect the vocal cords and promote overall vocal health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus serpyllum flowers at their peak fragrance and potency when the buds have just opened.
Dry the harvested flowers to a moisture content of 10-15% by spreading them out in a single layer.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a steam distillation apparatus at a temperature range of 100-110°c.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation device.
Filter the essential oil through a paper or cloth filter to remove any impurities and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with lost voice because of its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in relieving respiratory issues and soothing the throat. It contains compounds like camphor and borneol that help in breaking down mucus and reducing inflammation, thus allowing the vocal cords to recover and regain their natural function. Additionally, rosemary oil's antioxidant properties help in protecting the vocal cords from further damage and promoting a healthy recovery. This makes it a popular remedy for singers and public speakers.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: Acts as an expectorant and helps loosen mucus in the throat, allowing the voice to recover.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce throat inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthy vocal cord environment.
α-Pinene: Possesses bronchodilatory effects, which help open up airways and improve breathing, reducing vocal strain and promoting a healthy voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Rosmarinus officinalis for lost voice essential oils due to their high concentration of camphor and borneol, which help to soothe and calm the throat.
Flowers: The flowers are used to add a sweet and herbaceous note to the essential oil, which can help to uplift and calm the mind, as well as promote relaxation and reduce throat inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are also used to create essential oils for lost voice, as they contain a higher concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Steam distill the rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the distillation apparatus.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense essential oils helps with lost voice because of its soothing and calming properties. The oil, extracted from the White Clover plant, has anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce throat irritation and inflammation. Its expectorant properties aid in loosening mucus and promoting a smooth flow of air, allowing for easier vocalization. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that may be contributing to the loss of voice. This makes Trifolium pratense essential oils a natural remedy for soothing a sore throat and restoring a lost voice.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These plant-derived compounds may help alleviate vocal strain by reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant activity in the throat and laryngeal tissues.

Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could aid in soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the throat, thereby helping to recover lost voice.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the throat and laryngeal tissues, promoting a faster recovery from vocal strain and lost voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make essential oils as they are rich in saponins and other compounds that can help soothe and protect the vocal cords.
Flowers: They are used to make essential oils as they contain isoflavones, which can help to soothe and calm the throat, promoting a clear and strong voice.
Roots: They are used to make essential oils as they contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat and vocal cords.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh trifolium pratense flowers when they are in full bloom typically in mid to late summer.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers using a glass distillation apparatus for 2-3 hours to yield 10-15ml essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel and filter it through a paper filter.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place.
